subject: Ecology
title: What Makes an Island?
description: One-minute skill: What Makes an Island?.

hook.text: An island is any habitat surrounded by something its species can't easily cross.

buildup.text: Ocean islands are obvious, but mountaintops, lakes, and forest patches are islands too.

discovery.text: Isolation is the key ingredient. It limits who arrives and traps who stays.

twist.text: A city park surrounded by highways functions as an ecological island for ground-dwelling insects.

climax.text: Island biogeography applies far beyond the ocean—anywhere isolation shapes life.

punchline.text: Islands aren't just in the sea. They're everywhere.

quiz.question: What is the key ecological feature of an island?
